name: createApp()
description: Returns an application instance with Express-like routing, middleware support, WebSocket handling, error handling, and an HTTP/HTTPS server.

methods.description: Register global middleware; fn(req, res, next).
Register path-scoped middleware or mount a Router sub-app. The prefix is stripped from req.url.
Register GET route handlers. Optional opts object for { secure } matching.
Register POST route handlers.
Register PUT route handlers.
Register DELETE route handlers.
Register PATCH route handlers.
Register OPTIONS route handlers.
Register HEAD route handlers.
Register handlers for ALL HTTP methods.
Register a WebSocket upgrade handler. handler receives (ws, req).
Register a global error handler: fn(err, req, res, next). Only one at a time.
Start HTTP server, or HTTPS if tlsOpts includes { key, cert }. Returns the server instance.
Gracefully close the server and invoke the optional callback.
Return a flat array of all registered routes for introspection.
Bound request handler suitable for http.createServer(app.handler).

name: Router()
description: Creates a standalone router for modular route grouping. Supports all HTTP method helpers, route chaining via .route(), sub-router mounting via .use(), protocol-aware { secure } matching, and introspection via .routes().

methods.description: Register GET route. Optional { secure } option.
Register POST route.
Register PUT route.
Register DELETE route.
Register PATCH route.
Register OPTIONS route.
Register HEAD route.
Register route for all HTTP methods.
Register middleware or mount a nested sub-router.
Return a chainable object with .get(), .post(), etc. for a single path.
Return an array of all registered routes with method, path, and secure flag.

name: Request (req)
description: Wraps the incoming Node http.IncomingMessage with convenient properties and helpers. Includes HTTPS detection via req.secure and req.protocol.
example:
app.get('/info', (req, res) => {
	console.log(req.method)           // 'GET'
	console.log(req.url)              // '/info?foo=bar'
	console.log(req.query)            // { foo: 'bar' }
	console.log(req.ip)               // '127.0.0.1'
	console.log(req.secure)           // true/false
	console.log(req.protocol)         // 'https' or 'http'
	console.log(req.get('host'))      // 'localhost:3000'
	console.log(req.is('json'))       // false
	res.json({ ok: true })
})

methods.description: HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.).
Request URL path + query string.
Raw request headers (lowercased keys).
Parsed query string via URLSearchParams.
Route parameters populated by the router (e.g. { id: '42' }).
Parsed request body (null until a body parser runs).
Remote IP address from req.socket.remoteAddress.
true when the connection is over TLS (HTTPS).
'https' or 'http' based on connection type.
The underlying http.IncomingMessage.
Get a request header (case-insensitive).
Check if Content-Type contains the given type string (e.g. 'json', 'text/html').

name: Response (res)
description: Wraps the outgoing Node http.ServerResponse with chainable helpers for setting status, headers, sending responses, and opening an SSE stream.

methods.description: Set HTTP status code. Returns this (chainable).
Set a response header. Returns this (chainable).
Get a previously-set response header (case-insensitive).
Set Content-Type. Accepts shorthands: 'json', 'html', 'text', 'xml', 'form', 'bin'. Chainable.
Send the response. Auto-detects Content-Type: Buffer→octet-stream, '<…'→html, string→text, object→json.
Set Content-Type to application/json and send the object.
Set Content-Type to text/plain and send the string.
Set Content-Type to text/html and send the string.
Redirect to URL. Default status is 302.
Open a Server-Sent Events stream. Returns an SSEStream controller with send(), event(), comment(), close(), etc.

name: app.ws(path, [opts], handler)
description: Register a WebSocket upgrade handler on the application. Uses RFC 6455 framing with built-in ping/pong, sub-protocol negotiation, and client verification. The handler receives (ws, req) where ws is a WebSocketConnection with a rich event-driven API.

methods.description: Send a text or binary message. opts.binary forces a binary frame.
JSON-serialize and send as a text frame.
Send a ping frame (auto-pings are built-in via pingInterval).
Send a pong frame.
Initiate a graceful close with optional status code and reason.
Forcefully destroy the underlying socket.
Listen for events: 'message', 'close', 'error', 'ping', 'pong', 'drain'.
One-time event listener.
Remove a specific event listener.
Remove all listeners for an event, or all events.
Return the number of listeners for an event.

options.notes: Maximum incoming frame size in bytes. Frames exceeding this close the connection with 1009.
Auto-ping interval in ms. Set to 0 to disable.
fn(req) → boolean. Return false to reject the upgrade with 403 Forbidden.

name: WebSocketConnection
description: Represents a single WebSocket connection. Created automatically when a client upgrades to WebSocket at a registered ws() path. Provides an event-driven API with per-connection data store, connection metadata, and binary/text messaging.
example:
// Properties available on each ws connection:
app.ws('/demo', (ws, req) => {
	console.log(ws.id)             // 'ws_1_a3x9k'
	console.log(ws.readyState)     // 1 (OPEN)
	console.log(ws.protocol)       // negotiated sub-protocol
	console.log(ws.headers)        // upgrade request headers
	console.log(ws.ip)             // remote IP
	console.log(ws.query)          // parsed query params
	console.log(ws.url)            // upgrade URL
	console.log(ws.secure)         // true for wss://
	console.log(ws.connectedAt)    // timestamp of connection
	console.log(ws.uptime)         // ms since connected
	console.log(ws.bufferedAmount) // pending bytes

	// Per-connection data store
	ws.data.role = 'admin'
	if (ws.data.role === 'admin') ws.send('admin tools unlocked')
})

methods.description: Unique connection identifier (e.g. ws_1_l8x3k).
0=CONNECTING, 1=OPEN, 2=CLOSING, 3=CLOSED (mirrors the WebSocket spec).
Negotiated sub-protocol from the Sec-WebSocket-Protocol handshake.
Headers from the initial HTTP upgrade request.
Remote IP address of the WebSocket client.
Parsed query parameters from the upgrade URL.
Full upgrade request URL.
true when the connection is over TLS (WSS).
Requested WebSocket extensions header from the client.
Maximum payload size in bytes for this connection.
Timestamp (ms) when the connection was established.
Milliseconds since the connection opened (computed getter).
Number of bytes queued for transmission.
Arbitrary per-connection data store. Use ws.data.key = value for user data.

name: res.sse([opts]) / SSEStream
description: Opens a Server-Sent Events stream from a route handler. Returns an SSEStream controller with methods for sending events, comments, and controlling the stream lifecycle. Supports auto-incrementing IDs, keep-alive, retry hints, and per-stream data store.

methods.description: Send unnamed data event. Objects are auto-JSON-serialized.
Alias for send() with an object.
Send a named event (event: name\ndata: ...).
Send a comment line (: text). Useful for keep-alive or debugging.
Send a retry: ms hint to set the client's reconnection interval.
Start or restart a keep-alive timer that sends periodic comments.
Flush buffered data through proxies (writes 2 KB padding).
Close the SSE stream from the server side.
Listen for 'close' or 'error' events.
One-time event listener.
Remove a specific event listener.
Remove all listeners for an event, or all events.
Return the number of listeners for an event.

options.notes: Reconnection interval hint (ms) sent to the client on stream open.
Auto keep-alive comment interval in ms. 0 = disabled.
Comment text sent by the keep-alive timer.
Auto-increment event IDs starting from startId.
Starting value for auto-generated IDs.
Bytes of initial padding (helps flush proxy buffers on connect).
HTTP status code for the SSE response.
Additional response headers merged into the SSE response.

name: compress([opts])
description: Response compression middleware using brotli, gzip, or deflate based on the client's Accept-Encoding header. Brotli is preferred when available (Node ≥ 11.7). Automatically negotiates encoding, respects threshold, skips SSE streams, and supports a filter function to skip specific requests.

options.notes: Minimum response body size in bytes before compression is applied.
Compression level (1–9). Higher = smaller output, more CPU.
fn(req, res) → boolean. Return false to skip compression for the request.

name: json([opts])
description: Parses JSON request bodies and populates req.body. Skips non-matching Content-Types and calls next(). Supports requireSecure to enforce HTTPS.

options.notes: Maximum body size. Accepts bytes or unit strings like '100kb', '1mb', '1gb'.
When true, only accepts objects and arrays (rejects primitives).
Optional function passed to JSON.parse for custom reviving.
MIME type to match. Supports wildcards ('*/*') or a custom predicate function.
When true, rejects non-HTTPS requests with 403 'HTTPS required'.

name: urlencoded([opts])
description: Parses application/x-www-form-urlencoded bodies into req.body. Supports requireSecure.

options.notes: When true, supports rich nested bracket syntax (a[b]=1, a[]=1). When false, returns flat key/value pairs.
Maximum body size (bytes or unit string).
MIME type to match.
When true, rejects non-HTTPS requests with 403 'HTTPS required'.

name: text([opts])
description: Reads plain text request bodies into req.body as a string. Supports requireSecure.

options.notes: MIME matcher — uses wildcard by default so it matches text/plain, text/html, etc.
Maximum body size.
Character encoding used to decode the incoming bytes.
When true, rejects non-HTTPS requests with 403 'HTTPS required'.

name: raw([opts])
description: Receives raw bytes as a Buffer on req.body. Useful for binary payloads. Supports requireSecure.

options.notes: MIME type to match for the raw parser.
Maximum body size. Returns 413 if exceeded.
When true, rejects non-HTTPS requests with 403 'HTTPS required'.

name: multipart([opts])
description: Streaming multipart parser that writes file parts to disk and collects text fields. Sets req.body to { fields, files }. Supports requireSecure.

options.notes: Directory to store uploaded files. Relative paths resolve from process.cwd(). Created automatically if missing.
Maximum file size in bytes. Exceeding this aborts the upload and returns HTTP 413.
When true, rejects non-HTTPS requests with 403 'HTTPS required'.

name: static(rootPath, [opts])
description: Serves static files from a filesystem directory with Content-Type detection (60+ MIME types), dotfile policy, caching, extension fallback, and custom header hooks. Only handles GET and HEAD requests.

options.notes: File to serve for directory requests. Set false to disable auto-index.
Cache-Control max-age in milliseconds. Converted to seconds internally.
'allow' | 'deny' | 'ignore'. deny→403, ignore→skip to next middleware, allow→serve normally.
Fallback extensions to try when a file is not found (e.g. ['html', 'htm']).
Hook (res, filePath) => {} to set custom headers before streaming the file.

name: cors([opts])
description: Small CORS middleware. Handles preflight OPTIONS requests automatically with 204 and sets standard CORS headers on all responses. Supports suffix-based origin matching for subdomains.

options.notes: Allowed origins. '*' allows any. Array entries starting with '.' do suffix matching. Set falsy to disable.
Allowed HTTP methods for Access-Control-Allow-Methods.
Allowed headers for Access-Control-Allow-Headers.
When true and a specific origin matches, sets Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true.

name: fetch(url, [opts])
description: Small Node HTTP/HTTPS client returning a response with status, ok, statusText, secure, url, headers.get(), and helpers: text(), json(), arrayBuffer(). Supports timeouts, AbortSignal, progress callbacks, auto-serialization, and TLS options passthrough for HTTPS URLs.

options.notes: HTTP method (uppercased internally).
Request headers.
Request body. Plain objects are auto-JSON-encoded. URLSearchParams produce urlencoded bodies. Streams are piped.
Request timeout in milliseconds. Creates an ETIMEOUT error on expiry.
AbortController signal to cancel the request.
Optional agent for connection pooling or proxies.
Callback receiving { loaded, total } on each response chunk.
Callback receiving { loaded, total } during body upload.
TLS option: set false to accept self-signed certs.
TLS options: custom certificate authority, client cert, key, or PKCS12 bundle.
Passphrase for the private key or pfx.
SNI hostname for TLS negotiation.
TLS version constraints (e.g. 'TLSv1.2', 'TLSv1.3').

name: rateLimit([opts])
description: In-memory, per-IP rate-limiting middleware. Sets standard X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset headers on every response. Returns 429 JSON error when the limit is exceeded. Expired entries are cleaned up automatically.

options.notes: Time window in milliseconds (default: 1 minute).
Maximum requests per window per key.
HTTP status code for rate-limited responses.

options.default: Too many requests, please try again later.
options.notes: Error message returned in the JSON response body when limit is exceeded.
Custom function to extract a rate-limit key from the request.

name: logger([opts])
description: Request-logging middleware that prints method, URL, status code, and response time. Supports three output formats with optional ANSI colorization. Hooks into the response 'finish' event to capture final status.

options.notes: 'dev' (colorized method/status/time), 'short' (IP + method + url + status + time), or 'tiny' (method + url + status + time).
Custom log output function.
Enable or disable ANSI color codes. Auto-detected from process.stdout.isTTY.
